package repository

import (
	"myapp/service"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository imports and depends on the service package.
// This inverts the expected dependency direction: service -> repository, not repository -> service.

type Notification struct {
	ID      string `gorm:"column:id"`
	UserID  string `gorm:"column:user_id"`
	Message string `gorm:"column:message"`
}

type NotificationRepository struct {
	db            *gorm.DB
	emailService  *service.EmailService
	smsService    *service.SMSService
}

// NewNotificationRepository accepts service-layer dependencies - anti-pattern.
func NewNotificationRepository(db *gorm.DB, emailSvc *service.EmailService, smsSvc *service.SMSService) *NotificationRepository {
	return &NotificationRepository{
		db:           db,
		emailService: emailSvc,
		smsService:   smsSvc,
	}
}

// SaveAndNotify persists the notification and calls service methods directly.
func (r *NotificationRepository) SaveAndNotify(n *Notification) error {
	if err := r.db.Create(n).Error; err != nil {
		return err
	}

	// Repository calling service-layer methods - inverted dependency.
	r.emailService.Send(n.UserID, n.Message)
	r.smsService.Send(n.UserID, n.Message)
	return nil
}

// GetUserPreferences delegates to service instead of returning raw data.
func (r *NotificationRepository) GetUserPreferences(userID string) *service.UserPreferences {
	return service.GetUserPreferences(userID)
}
